reforms.description: The Swedish government has implemented significant reforms in the education system, including the introduction of educational vouchers to enhance competition among schools and improve labor market incentives for both employers and employees. Additionally, there is a focus on enhancing adult and tertiary education to better align with labor market needs and consumer preferences.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and labor market incentives is expected to enhance productivity and adaptability in the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been fully implemented as part of the 1996 Employment Bill.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in the education system and labor market policies.
reforms.source_quote: The government is relying heavily on education and training – specifically policies to promote adult and tertiary education – to eventually ensure the compatibility of present levels of labour costs with full employment.

reforms.description: The Swedish government has adopted various policies to support the startup ecosystem, including initiatives to promote entrepreneurship and support small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through tax relief and reduced labor market rigidities. Additionally, ALMI Business Partner and regional development companies provide consultation and financing to new business ventures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting SMEs and entrepreneurship is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has successfully implemented various measures to support SMEs and entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant commitment to enhancing the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The ALMI Business Partner company, which is owned by the government, and 22 regional development companies owned by county councils, offer consultation, financing and information to persons planning to start a new business.

reforms.description: The Swedish government is considering expanding higher education to better produce highly-qualified labor that meets the evolving demands of the labor market and consumer preferences. Additionally, there are discussions about devolving some business-sector related policies to regional and local authorities to enhance local entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding higher education is expected to improve the skill set of the workforce, thereby enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is in the process of considering these reforms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of higher education is significant but not as transformative as the major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: One element of the action plan which is still under consideration is the tentative devolution of some business-sector related policies to the regional and local authorities.
